name: Free-Energy Minimizing Homeostatic Agent (Probabilistic/Bayesian)
description: Active inference formulation where the agent maintains a probabilistic belief (Gaussian posterior) over its internal hunger state and a belief map over resource locations. Actions are selected by minimizing expected free energy, which decomposes into a pragmatic term (expected homeostatic drive reduction — connecting to Keramati & Gutkin 2014's equivalence between drive and negative log-probability per Friston 2010) and an epistemic term (information gain about resource locations, driving exploration of uncertain grid cells). This unifies homeostatic regulation with Bayesian inference and active exploration in a single principled framework. Based on Friston (2010) Free Energy Principle, Keramati & Gutkin (2014) postulates P1 and P2, and Petzschner et al. (2021) on computational interoception.

variables.name: true_hunger
variables.description: True internal hunger state. Not directly observed — the agent receives a noisy interoceptive observation. Updated via metabolic drift and consumption.

variables.name: believed_hunger_mean
variables.description: Mean of the agent's Gaussian posterior belief over its current hunger level. Updated each step via Bayesian fusion of prior prediction and noisy observation.

variables.name: believed_hunger_variance
variables.description: Variance of the agent's Gaussian posterior belief over hunger. Decreases with observations, increases with process noise during prediction.

variables.name: hunger_observation
variables.description: Noisy interoceptive observation of hunger. Simulated as h_t + Gaussian noise with std sigma_obs. In the simulation, derived from the true h_t with added noise.

variables.name: resource_belief_map
variables.description: A grid_width × grid_height map where each cell holds P(food present at cell). Initialized to a uniform prior (count/total_cells). Updated when cells are observed directly.
variables.initial_value: uniform prior based on food count / total cells

variables.name: pragmatic_value
variables.description: Pragmatic (drive-reducing) component of expected free energy for action a. Negative expected drive after taking action a: favors actions that reduce expected hunger deviation from setpoint.

variables.name: epistemic_value
variables.description: Epistemic (uncertainty-reducing) component of expected free energy for action a. Sum of binary entropies of resource beliefs for cells that would become newly visible after action a. Higher = more information gain.

variables.name: expected_free_energy
variables.description: Total expected free energy for action a: G(a) = G_prag(a) + w_e * G_epist(a). Higher is better (we maximize G, or equivalently minimize -G).

variables.name: ate_food_flag
variables.description: Boolean (0 or 1) indicating successful food consumption this step.

parameters.name: setpoint_prior_mean
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2014): ideal = no deficit; also the mean of the preferred-state prior p(h) in Friston (2010)

parameters.name: prior_std
parameters.source: Friston (2010): tighter prior = stronger homeostatic regulation; controls width of preferred-state distribution

parameters.name: interoceptive_noise_std
parameters.source: Petzschner et al. (2021): interoceptive uncertainty in hunger perception

parameters.name: process_noise_variance
parameters.source: Petzschner et al. (2021): uncertainty accumulation during prediction step

parameters.name: action_selection_inv_temperature
parameters.source: Controls policy determinism in action selection

parameters.name: resource_belief_decay
parameters.source: Rate at which unvisited cells lose believed resource probability when observed empty

parameters.name: initial_resource_belief
parameters.source: Prior probability of food at each cell; ≈ food_count / total_cells = 5/100

rules.description: Prediction step — propagate belief forward with metabolic drift and process noise
rules.pseudocode:
mu_h_prior = mu_h + lambda_drift
sigma_h_sq_prior = sigma_h_sq + sigma_process_sq

rules.description: Generate noisy interoceptive observation (simulation-side, not agent-side)
rules.pseudocode:
o_h = h_t + random_normal(0, sigma_obs)
# In implementation: the agent uses its own h_t + noise as the observation

rules.description: Bayesian belief update — fuse prior prediction with noisy observation
rules.pseudocode:
# Kalman-style Gaussian fusion
K_gain = sigma_h_sq_prior / (sigma_h_sq_prior + sigma_obs ** 2)
mu_h = mu_h_prior + K_gain * (o_h - mu_h_prior)
sigma_h_sq = (1 - K_gain) * sigma_h_sq_prior

rules.description: Pragmatic value — negative expected drive under action a (drive = squared deviation / sigma_p^2)
rules.pseudocode:
# For eat action (if food present):
mu_after_eat = mu_h + lambda_drift - K
G_prag_eat = -((mu_after_eat - h_star) ** 2 + sigma_h_sq) / (sigma_p ** 2)

# For non-eat actions:
mu_after_move = mu_h + lambda_drift
G_prag_move = -((mu_after_move - h_star) ** 2 + sigma_h_sq) / (sigma_p ** 2)

# For stay:
G_prag_stay = G_prag_move  # same as movement (no eating)
rules.source_postulate: P1, P2; Friston (2010) drive ≡ negative log-prior

rules.description: Epistemic value — information gain about resource locations from visiting new cells
rules.pseudocode:
# Binary entropy: H(p) = -p * ln(p) - (1-p) * ln(1-p)
def binary_entropy(p):
    p = clip(p, 1e-9, 1 - 1e-9)
    return -p * log(p) - (1 - p) * log(1 - p)

# For movement action a: compute position after action
new_pos = (clip(x + dx, 0, grid_w-1), clip(y + dy, 0, grid_h-1))

# Epistemic value = entropy of resource belief at the destination cell
# (observing that cell will resolve uncertainty)
G_epist_a = binary_entropy(R_beliefs[new_pos[0]][new_pos[1]])

# For eat and stay: no new cell observed
G_epist_eat = 0.0
G_epist_stay = 0.0

rules.description: Softmax action selection over expected free energy (maximize G)
rules.pseudocode:
all_actions = ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right', 'stay', 'eat']
G_vals = [G_a for a in all_actions]  # computed via R4+R5+R6

# If no food at position, set G(eat) = -1e9
if not food_at_position:
    G_vals[eat_index] = -1e9

max_G = max(G_vals)
exp_vals = [exp(beta_G * (g - max_G)) for g in G_vals]
sum_exp = sum(exp_vals)
probs = [e / sum_exp for e in exp_vals]
a_t = random_choice(all_actions, probs)
h_t = clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K * ate_t, 0, h_max)

rules.description: Resource belief map update based on observation
rules.pseudocode:
# For current position: check if food is actually there
current_cell = (perception['x'], perception['y'])
food_here = any(f['x'] == current_cell[0] and f['y'] == current_cell[1] for f in food_list)

if food_here:
    R_beliefs[current_cell[0]][current_cell[1]] = 1.0
else:
    # Decay belief for visited empty cell
    R_beliefs[current_cell[0]][current_cell[1]] *= (1 - kappa)

decision_logic.description: The agent maintains a Gaussian belief over its hunger state (mu_h, sigma_h_sq) and a grid-wide resource belief map. Each step: (1) predict hunger forward via metabolic drift + process noise, (2) generate a noisy interoceptive observation and fuse it with the prediction via Bayesian update, (3) update resource beliefs based on what is currently visible, (4) for each candidate action compute pragmatic value (expected drive reduction) and epistemic value (information gain about resources), (5) combine into expected free energy G(a), (6) select action via softmax over G. After acting, update the true internal hunger state. This agent explores uncertain areas when hunger is low and exploits known food sources when hunger is high, with smooth transitions governed by the free energy decomposition.
pseudocode: food_positions = set((f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list)
# Step 2: Noisy observation and Bayesian update (R2, R3)
o_h = h_t + random_normal(0, sigma_obs)  # simulated interoceptive noise
sigma_h_sq = max((1 - K_gain) * sigma_h_sq_prior, 0.001)  # floor to avoid zero variance
# Epistemic: entropy of resource belief at destination
exp_vals = [exp(beta_G * (g - max_G)) for g in G_vals]
probs = [e / sum_exp for e in exp_vals]
ate_t = 1 if (action == 'eat' and new_perception.get('last_action_result', {}).get('consumed', False)) else 0
h_t = clip(h_t + lambda_drift - K * ate_t, 0, h_max)
# mu_h and sigma_h_sq persist for next step's prediction phase
# R_beliefs persists and will be updated next step

perception_to_variables.food_positions: set((f['x'], f['y']) for f in perception['resources'].get('food', []))
perception_to_variables.o_h (hunger observation): h_t + random_normal(0, sigma_obs) — agent self-generates noisy observation from its own true h_t

env_mapping.reward_source: Internally computed via expected free energy G(a). The environment reward from ConsumeEffect (reward=1.0) is NOT used — the agent is driven entirely by free energy minimization over its believed internal state and resource uncertainty.

expected_behaviors.description: Agent explores uncertain areas when hunger is low (epistemic drive dominates)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_t ≈ 0 (sated), initialize R_beliefs with high entropy in distant cells → assert agent moves toward high-entropy cells rather than known food

expected_behaviors.description: Agent exploits known food when hungry (pragmatic drive dominates)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set h_t = 7.0, R_beliefs[food_cell] = 1.0 → assert agent moves toward food_cell and eats

expected_behaviors.description: Bayesian belief tracks true hunger with interoceptive noise filtered out
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 100 steps → assert abs(mu_h - h_t) < 2 * sigma_obs on average (belief is calibrated)

expected_behaviors.description: Eating near setpoint yields near-zero pragmatic benefit (alliesthesia via free energy)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set mu_h ≈ 0.1, compute G_prag(eat) vs G_prag(stay) → assert difference is very small

expected_behaviors.description: Resource beliefs decay for empty cells, persist for food cells
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: visit empty cell 20 times → assert R_beliefs[cell] → 0; visit food cell → assert R_beliefs[cell] = 1.0

expected_behaviors.description: Smooth transition from exploration to exploitation as hunger rises
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: sweep h_t from 0 to 10 → plot P(move_toward_known_food) vs P(move_toward_uncertain_cell) → assert crossover occurs

references: Friston, K. (2010). The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory? Nature Reviews Neuroscience.
Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2014). Homeostatic reinforcement learning for integrating reward collection and physiological stability. eLife 3:e04811.
Petzschner, F.H., Garfinkel, S.N., Paulus, M.P., Koch, C. & Khalsa, S.S. (2021). Computational Models of Interoception and Body Regulation. Trends in Neurosciences 44(1): 63-76.
Cabanac, M. (1971). Physiological role of pleasure. Science.
